(4) Extract spike waveform features using PCA or wavelet decomposition. (5) Cluster spike waveforms using automated methods (KiloSort-style template matching or Gaussian mixture models). (6) Evaluate cluster quality: ISI violations, isolation distance, L-ratio, silhouette score. (7) Merge over-split clusters and remove noise clusters. (8) Generate unit summary plots: mean waveforms, ISI histograms, autocorrelograms. (9) Export spike times and cluster assignments for each sorted unit.

name: Neural Decoding
description: Decode behavioral variables or stimuli from neural population activity
prompt: Build a neural decoder from the uploaded neural data. Steps: (1) Load the neural data (spike trains or calcium traces) and the behavioral variable to decode: {decode_target}. (2) Align neural data to behavioral events and bin spike counts at {bin_size} ms resolution. (3) Split data into training (80%) and test (20%) sets, preserving temporal structure. (4) Train decoders: Naive Bayes, linear discriminant analysis, SVM, and/or recurrent neural network. (5) Evaluate decoding accuracy using cross-validation and confusion matrices. (6) Identify the most informative neurons using weight analysis or feature importance. (7) Analyze how decoding accuracy changes with neuron count and time bin size. (8) Visualize decoded vs actual trajectories and confusion matrices. (9) Export decoder performance and informative neuron rankings.

placeholders.label: Variable to decode (e.g., movement direction, stimulus identity, position)

name: Brain Connectivity Analysis
description: Map structural and functional connectivity networks in the brain
prompt: Analyze brain connectivity from the uploaded neuroimaging data. Steps: (1) Load the data and define brain regions/parcellation ({atlas}). (2) For functional connectivity: compute the correlation (or partial correlation) matrix between all ROI pairs. (3) For structural connectivity: extract streamline counts between regions from tractography. (4) Threshold the connectivity matrix to create a binary network. (5) Compute graph-theoretic metrics: degree, clustering coefficient, betweenness centrality, modularity, small-world index, global efficiency. (6) Identify hub regions and network modules (communities). (7) Compare connectivity between groups ({groups}) using network-based statistics. (8) Visualize the connectome as a circle plot and 3D brain network. (9) Export the connectivity matrix, network metrics, and hub list.

name: Behavioral Data Analysis
description: Analyze behavioral tasks: reaction times, accuracy, learning curves, and strategy
prompt: Analyze the uploaded behavioral dataset from: {task}. Steps: (1) Load the trial-by-trial behavioral data (reaction times, accuracy, choices). (2) Compute summary statistics per condition: mean RT, accuracy, and variability. (3) Clean reaction times: remove anticipatory responses (<100 ms) and timeouts. (4) Analyze accuracy as a function of condition using logistic mixed-effects models. (5) Analyze reaction times using linear mixed-effects models accounting for subject-level variability. (6) Model learning curves or adaptation effects over trials. (7) If applicable, fit computational models (drift diffusion, reinforcement learning) to the choice data. (8) Visualize psychometric functions, RT distributions, and learning curves. (9) Export statistical results and model parameters.

placeholders.label: Behavioral task name (e.g., Stroop, n-back, probabilistic learning)

name: Calcium Imaging Analysis
description: Process and analyze two-photon or one-photon calcium imaging data
prompt: Analyze the uploaded calcium imaging dataset. Steps: (1) Load the imaging data (TIFF stack or extracted traces). (2) If raw images: perform motion correction to stabilize the field of view. (3) Detect ROIs (neurons) using constrained non-negative matrix factorization (CNMF) or suite2p-style cell detection. (4) Extract fluorescence traces (ΔF/F₀) for each detected cell. (5) Deconvolve calcium traces to estimate spike times. (6) Compute tuning properties: align neural responses to {stimulus_or_behavior} and calculate selectivity metrics. (7) Identify ensemble activity patterns and correlated neuron groups. (8) Visualize spatial maps of tuning properties, population activity heatmaps, and example traces. (9) Export extracted traces, spike times, and tuning metrics.

placeholders.label: Stimulus or behavior to relate neural activity to

name: Neuropixels Data Processing
description: Process high-density Neuropixels recordings: spike sorting and unit analysis
prompt: Process the uploaded Neuropixels recording. Steps: (1) Load the raw Neuropixels data (AP and LFP bands) and inspect the probe geometry. (2) Apply preprocessing: common median referencing, bandpass filtering, and bad channel detection. (3) Run spike sorting to isolate single units. (4) Curate sorted units: merge over-split clusters, remove noise, label multi-unit activity. (5) Map unit positions on the probe to brain regions using the insertion coordinates and {atlas}. (6) Compute basic unit properties: firing rate, waveform shape, burst index, ISI statistics. (7) Analyze LFP: compute spectrograms and current source density (CSD) profiles. (8) Visualize the drift map, unit quality metrics, and brain region assignments. (9) Export the sorted spike data and unit metadata.

placeholders.label: Brain atlas for region mapping (e.g., Allen CCF)

name: Brain Atlas Mapping
description: Register neural data to brain atlases and visualize anatomical context
prompt: Map the uploaded neural data to a brain atlas. Steps: (1) Load the data and determine the recording modality (imaging, electrophysiology, tracing). (2) Select the reference atlas: {atlas} (e.g., Allen Mouse CCF, MNI152 human). (3) Register the data to atlas coordinates using landmarks, fiducials, or automated registration. (4) Assign brain regions to each data point (electrodes, neurons, voxels, or injection sites). (5) Compute the regional distribution of the signal of interest. (6) Generate atlas-overlay visualizations showing data mapped onto anatomical sections. (7) Create a 3D rendering of recorded locations within the brain. (8) Summarize the regional distribution in a table. (9) Export registered coordinates and atlas-annotated data.

placeholders.label: Brain atlas (e.g., Allen CCF, MNI152, Paxinos-Watson)

name: Computational Neuron Model
description: Build and simulate biophysical or integrate-and-fire neuron models
prompt: Build and simulate a computational neuron model. Model type: {model_type}. Steps: (1) Define the neuron model parameters: membrane capacitance, conductances, reversal potentials, and morphology if applicable. (2) Implement the model equations (Hodgkin-Huxley, Izhikevich, AdEx, or LIF as specified). (3) Simulate the neuron's response to: {input_protocol} (e.g., step current, ramp, synaptic input, noise). (4) Record membrane potential, firing rate, and any tracked currents. (5) Compute the F-I curve (firing rate vs injected current). (6) Analyze spike shape, adaptation, and bursting properties. (7) Perform parameter sensitivity analysis on key biophysical parameters. (8) Visualize voltage traces, phase plots, and the F-I curve. (9) Export model parameters and simulation data.

placeholders.label: Model type (e.g., Hodgkin-Huxley, Izhikevich, LIF, AdEx)

name: Psychophysics Analysis
description: Analyze psychophysical data: thresholds, psychometric functions, and signal detection
prompt: Analyze the uploaded psychophysics dataset from: {experiment}. Steps: (1) Load the trial-by-trial data with stimulus levels and responses. (2) Fit a psychometric function (logistic, Weibull, or cumulative Gaussian) to the data. (3) Estimate the detection/discrimination threshold at {criterion}% correct. (4) Compute confidence intervals for the threshold using bootstrap. (5) If signal detection theory applies, compute d' (sensitivity) and criterion (c) for each condition. (6) Generate ROC curves if rating data is available. (7) Compare thresholds or sensitivity across conditions ({conditions}) using appropriate statistical tests. (8) Visualize psychometric functions and SDT metrics. (9) Export thresholds, psychometric function parameters, and SDT metrics.

placeholders.label: Experiment type (e.g., contrast detection, orientation discrimination)

name: Neural Network vs Brain Comparison
description: Compare artificial neural network representations with neural recordings
prompt: Compare artificial neural network representations with brain data. Steps: (1) Load the neural data (fMRI, electrophysiology, or behavioral) and the ANN model: {model_name}. (2) Present the same stimuli ({stimuli}) to both the ANN and extract layer activations. (3) Compute representational dissimilarity matrices (RDMs) for each ANN layer and each brain region/timepoint. (4) Compare ANN and brain RDMs using representational similarity analysis (RSA) — Spearman correlation or Kendall's tau. (5) Determine which ANN layer best predicts each brain region. (6) Fit encoding models: predict neural responses from ANN features using ridge regression. (7) Evaluate encoding model accuracy with cross-validated R². (8) Visualize the layer-region correspondence and encoding model performance. (9) Export RSA matrices and encoding model results.
